name: Cache-Oblivious Algorithms
def: Algorithms that achieve optimal cache use across all cache sizes without knowing the parameters.
problem: Get good locality on any memory hierarchy without per-machine tuning of block sizes.
mechanism: Recursive divide-and-conquer keeps shrinking the working set so it eventually fits each cache level automatically (e.g. cache-oblivious matrix multiply).
limits: Often beaten by hand-tuned blocking on a known machine
whenNot: When you can tune cache-blocking for the exact target
myths: "Cache-oblivious beats cache-aware" — tuned blocking usually wins on a known machine
